metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5183
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1a, S1: generation of a polyclonal pan-DCLK-antibody against the DCLK1 DCX domain. Targets a region with high homology between DCLK1 and DCLK2, has higher affinity for GFP-DCLK1 than GFP-DCLK2, and was validated with DCLK1-/- and DCLK2-/- knockout mouse controls.

Figure 1i-j: immunofluorescence staining for endogenous PSD95 (DLG4) and overexpressed GFP-DCLK1 in cultured hippocampal neurons showed DCLK1 localization to dendrites and dendritic spines.

Figure 1k: DCLK was enriched in SV, synaptic plasma membrane and PSD biochemical fractions.

Figure 1l: pulldown of anti-DCLK in adult brain yields PSD95 (DLG4).. Experimental description: Mice were used throughout the study; knockouts, shRNA and GFP contructs.

"Golgi staining of the hippocampal CA1 area prepared from wild-type or DCLK1-/- mice"

"The cDNA of mouse wild-type DCLK1 sequence was amplified by PCR and inserted into pEGFP-N1 (Clontech) to generate DCLK1-GFP"

RNAi:
"The RNAi-targeting sequence for DCLK1 (5'-GGTTCGATTCTACAGA AAT-3') was as previously reported17. The targeting sequence for DCLK2 (5'-GCAGGTTACTTGTCTGCAAGA-3') was designed ab initio and its effect was confirmed by co-transfection with DCLK2-GFP. These oligonucleotides were ligated into the shRNA expression vector (pSilencer 2.0-U6; Invitrogen) and transfected into cells using the Effectene transfection reagent (Qiagen). Stealth RNAi Negative Control Duplexes (Invitrogen) were used as control shRNAs. The sequence for a resistant form of DCLK1 was designed by introducing three-point mutations into the original sequences (5'-GGTCCGATTTTACAGG AAT-3')."
